source.title: Control and Prevention of Popcorn Polymer Hazard at Port Neches, Texas

actions_taken: Emergency response by TPC Group fire team and Port Neches Fire Department
Investigation by U.S. Chemical Safety and Hazard Investigation Board (CSB)
Investigation by Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)
event.summary: On November 27, 2019, a dangerous substance known as popcorn polymer accumulated in a temporary dead leg created when a process pump was taken out of service for maintenance at the TPC Group Chemical Plant in Port Neches, Texas. The popcorn polymer expanded and ruptured the piping, releasing butadiene. The butadiene release caused fires that burned for more than a month, destroyed the butadiene unit, caused extensive facility damage, and resulted in at least five local residents reporting injuries. The incident resulted in $45 million in on-site property damage and $9.6 million in off-site property damage.
recommendations: Ensure that piping associated with out-of-service equipment in high-purity butadiene service be flushed monthly
Identify, control, or prevent all potential temporary dead legs within the unit

Develop and implement an effective safety management system to identify when safety-critical procedures cannot be conducted as intended or to identify associated safety implications when procedures cannot be implemented

hazards.name: Popcorn polymer formation in dead leg piping

threats.name: Accumulation of popcorn polymer in temporary dead leg during pump maintenance
Continued expansion of popcorn polymer in confined piping section
Pressure buildup from polymer expansion exceeding piping design limits

consequences.name: Fires burning for extended duration within facility
Property damage to on-site and off-site locations

controls.name: Procedure to minimize popcorn polymer hazards associated with dead legs
controls.barrier_role: Prevent accumulation of hazardous popcorn polymer

supporting_text: TPC Group had a procedure in place to minimize popcorn polymer hazards associated with dead legs
The CSB found no evidence that the inability to implement some of the procedure requirements when the pump was taken out of service triggered any type of safety evaluation

controls.name: Monthly flushing of piping associated with out-of-service equipment
controls.barrier_role: Prevent polymer accumulation through regular maintenance

supporting_text: CSB recommendation: ensure that piping associated with out-of-service equipment in high-purity butadiene service be flushed monthly

controls.name: Identification and control of all potential temporary dead legs
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate or control dead leg creation during maintenance

supporting_text: A temporary dead leg was created when a process pump was taken out of service for maintenance
CSB recommendation: identify, control, or prevent all potential temporary dead legs within the unit

controls.name: Safety management system to evaluate procedure implementation constraints
controls.barrier_role: Identify safety implications when safety-critical procedures cannot be implemented

supporting_text: TPC Group did not have an effective safety management system in place to identify when the safety-critical procedure could not be conducted as intended
The CSB found no evidence that the inability to implement some of the procedure requirements triggered any type of safety evaluation

controls.name: Pressure relief or rupture protection on dead leg piping
controls.barrier_role: Prevent or limit pressure buildup from polymer expansion

supporting_text: The piping ruptured when internal pressure from popcorn polymer expansion exceeded design limits

controls.name: Emergency response and fire suppression systems
controls.barrier_role: Contain and suppress fires resulting from butadiene release

supporting_text: TPC Group fire team, along with Port Neches Fire Department and Sabine-Neches Chiefs Association, responded to the incident
Fires burned for more than a month within the facility

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
